stories.title: CPSC Recall Alert — VEVOR Baby Swings and a Wave of Infant Product Recalls

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
⚠️ RECALL ALERT: VEVOR baby swings (models BB501K, BB702A, BB005K) are recalled for suffocation risk.

If you bought one on Amazon or Vevor.com between Jan–Aug 2025, stop using it now. Full refund available.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
VEVOR baby swing recalled: 3 steps

1. Stop using immediately
2. Cut the cover in half, write "Recalled" on base
3. Email recalling@vevor.com for full refund

Models BB501K, BB702A, BB005K (Amazon, Jan–Aug 2025).

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

research.facts:
VEVOR Baby Swing Recall + CPSC Safety Wave
**Sources:**
- CPSC official recall notice: VEVOR/Sanven Technology recall (recall number: 25-143)
- Additional CPSC active recalls: Joyful Journeys Baby Loungers, CooCooBaby Baby Loungers, Babypark Children's Carriers (July 2, 2026), AMASKY Nursing Pillows
**Verified Facts:**
- Brand: VEVOR (company: Sanven Technology Co. Ltd.)
- Units recalled: approximately 1,020
- Sold: Amazon and Vevor.com, January–August 2025
- Price range: $65–$80
- Models affected: BB501K, BB702A, BB005K (HIGH confidence — CPSC notice)
- Hazard: incline angle exceeds 10 degrees, violating Safe Sleep for Babies Act (federal law banning inclined infant sleepers)
- Risk: suffocation
- Remedy: STOP USING immediately; cut the swing cover in half; write "Recalled" on base; photograph it; email recalling@vevor.com for full refund (HIGH confidence — CPSC notice)
- Phone: 855-599-6320 (HIGH confidence — CPSC notice)
- Broader CPSC wave: multiple concurrent infant product recalls in 2026 (HIGH — CPSC records)
- Joyful Journeys Baby Loungers (inclined sleeper violation)
- CooCooBaby Baby Loungers (inclined sleeper violation)
- Babypark Children's Carriers (July 2, 2026)
- AMASKY Nursing Pillows (4,008 units)
**Confidence Assessment:**
- All product/recall details: HIGH (CPSC official source)
- Unit counts for secondary recalls: MEDIUM (noted where single-source)
- Legal basis (Safe Sleep for Babies Act): HIGH (federal statute)

stories.title: Pikes Peak or Bust Rodeo — Family Guide for the Final Days (Through July 18)

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
The Pikes Peak or Bust Rodeo is open through Saturday!

Mutton Bustin': ages 4–9, under 50 lbs, $20/ride at Norris Penrose Event Center.

Plus: pony rides, petting zoo, face painting. Tickets $25–$50. 🤠

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Pikes Peak or Bust Rodeo — since 1946, proceeds have supported local military families.

Runs through Saturday at 1045 Lower Gold Camp Road. Tickets at pikespeakorbust.org.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

stories.title: Colorado's Student Phone Law Is in Effect — What COS Parents Need to Know Before August

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
Colorado's student phone law is in effect.

All COS schools must post their device policy online by July 1. D11 already uses phone pouches all day.

D20 and D49 families: find your school's policy before August.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:
HB25-1135 requires phone bans — but schools must still exempt kids with disabilities, IEPs, 504 plans, or medical monitoring devices.

Know the rule. Know the exceptions. Back-to-school is weeks away.

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

research.facts:
Colorado Student Phone Law HB25-1135
**Sources:**
- Colorado General Assembly HB25-1135 (official bill text)
- Colorado Department of Education guidance
- D11 coverage (local news, school district official)
- EdWeek / national coverage of state phone laws
**Verified Facts:**
- Law: Colorado HB25-1135 (HIGH)
- Key deadline: all public and charter schools required to post cellphone/device policy on their website by July 1, 2026 (HIGH — bill text)
- D11 early adoption: implemented pouch system (phones stored in pouches all school day) starting 2024-25 school year (HIGH — district announcement)
- Statewide stats:
- ~89% of elementary schools have bell-to-bell phone bans (MEDIUM — state survey single-source)
- ~84% of middle schools have bell-to-bell phone bans (MEDIUM — state survey single-source)
- ~22% of high schools have bell-to-bell phone bans (MEDIUM — state survey single-source)
- Required exemptions: students with disabilities, IEPs, 504 plans, medical monitoring devices (HIGH — bill text)
- COS school return: D11, D20, D49 all returning in August 2026 (HIGH — district calendars)
**Confidence Assessment:**
- Law existence and key provisions: HIGH
- Exact percentages: MEDIUM (state survey, single source cited)
- D11 pouch system: HIGH

stories.title: Portal Pool Is Years Away — Here's Where COS Families Can Swim This Summer

Portal Pool is years away from reopening — a 1945 coal mine shaft runs beneath the park.

Here's where COS families can swim this summer: Monument Valley Pool, Wilson Ranch Pool, Briargate Splash Park, and more. 🏊

#COSParenting #ColoradoSprings #COSKids #PikesPeakParenting

research.facts:
Portal Pool Closure + Where to Swim in COS
**Sources:**
- Colorado Springs Gazette (May 2026 report)
- Colorado Springs Parks, Recreation and Cultural Services (pool/spray park hours)
- D11 free lunch program documentation
**Verified Facts:**
- Portal Pool status: CLOSED (HIGH — Gazette, Parks Dept)
- Reason: coal mine shaft running beneath Portal Park (HIGH — Gazette)
- Mine depth in some locations: as little as 25 feet below surface (HIGH — Gazette)
- Mine closure date: 1945 (HIGH — Gazette)
- Issues: roof collapses and leaks compromised pool and adjacent Memorial Park Family Center YMCA (HIGH — Gazette)
- Geotechnical investigation: scheduled to begin by end of 2026 (MEDIUM — single source, Gazette)
- Timeline for pool return: "years away" (HIGH — official characterization)
**Swimming alternatives (COS):**
- Monument Valley Pool: 220 Mesa Road, Mon–Sat 10 AM–6 PM, Sun noon–6 PM (HIGH — Parks Dept)
- Wilson Ranch Pool: 2335 Allegheny Drive (HIGH — Parks Dept)
- Briargate Splash Park: daily 10 AM–6 PM through September 7 (HIGH — Parks Dept)
- Deerfield Hills Spray Ground: daily 10 AM–5 PM (HIGH — Parks Dept)
- The Watering Hole at Venezia Park: daily 10 AM–6 PM, universally accessible (HIGH — Parks Dept)
- Uncle Wilber Fountain at Acacia Park: daily noon–6 PM, FREE (HIGH — Parks Dept)
- D11 free lunch for kids 0–18: East Library, 5550 N. Union Blvd., weekdays 11 AM–noon through July 31 (HIGH — D11)
**Confidence Assessment:**
- Pool closure and reason: HIGH
- Investigation timeline: MEDIUM (one source)
- Pool/spray park hours and addresses: HIGH (Parks Dept official)
- D11 lunch program: HIGH
